summary: Window has odd borders and fullscreens instead of maximizing when mail.tabs.drawInTitlebar = true
bug_report_text: Created attachment 9024886 20181114-010643.png User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:63.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/63.0 Steps to reproduce: I upgraded to Thunderbird 60.3.0 on Windows 10. Actual results: The Thunderbird window has an odd non-interactive border around it that is composed of glitched versions of other sections of the UI. Maximizing the Thunderbird window results in thunderbird going "full screen", maximizing over the task bar. Expected results: The thunderbird window has no border when maximized and no additional border apart from the Windows one. Maximizing Thunderbird should behave like any other program, not hiding the task bar.

summary: Add an overflow menu for toolbar to avoid buttons becoming invisible/unusable
bug_report_text: Created attachment 9428528 mozilla overflow 1.png User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:131.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/131.0 Steps to reproduce: Added some buttons to the toolbar (screenshot 1). Actual results: When resizing the window to make it fit a smaller screen, then on the right side of the toolbar the buttons disappeared behind the menu button and were not visible/usable anymore. Expected results: When the window is resized to a smaller size, instead of disappearing and becoming unusable, there should be a overflow menu like in Firefox (screenshot 2). This would make the buttons stay usable.

summary: Focused and pressed state of buttons on quick filter bar almost undiscoverable, and should be flat & borderless when unpressed on Windows
bug_report_text:
Seen on 112.0a1 (2023-02-20) (64-bit)

Expanded from Elizabeth's accessibility analysis

New 3-pane makes all the buttons on quick filter bar focusable - that's great. However, their focused or pressed state is almost undiscoverable and needs some love. Also, all of these buttons should be flat & borderless on Windows when not pressed (as on TB 102).

Richard?

Actual (see screenshot):
- Focused button not discoverable (`Starred` button has focus with very faint grey border)
- Pressed button not discoverable (`Tags` button is pressed, but hardly distinct from others)
- Unpressed buttons stand out from the entire flat UI with needless border
- `Any of/All of` selector looks alien

Expected
- Clear and consistent app-wide focus distinction (thick blue border)
- Clear contrast between unpressed (flat, borderless) vs. pressed (can then keep the inside shadow) as in TB 102.
- Unpressed buttons should be flat and borderless (as seen on TB 102) for
  - less saliency when unpressed (calm UI)
  - better distinction against pressed state
  - ux-consistency with our overall flat design (e.g. buttons on Places toolbar)
  - ux-consistency with Windows OS
- Make `Any of/All of` selector blend in better (font-size, border, etc.)

summary: Delete or collect PWMGR_MANAGE_* telemetry histograms
bug_report_text: In bug 1569253 we are removing passwordManager.js which has been forked to c-c (bug 1569113) and wanted to remove the probes from Histograms.json but noticed that TB didn't remove the histogram code nor did it add the probes to `"products"` for the probes. If we were to remove the entries from Histograms.json I believe it will cause runtime errors in the code trying to access it. Please either add TB to the products for the probes or remove the code referring to them. Thanks.

summary: If Message filters dialog height exceeds the height of the smaller of two monitors it is displayed on dialog is truncated on the small monitor
bug_report_text: Created attachment 9192866 FilterWindow.png +++ This bug was initially created as a clone of Bug #1633306 +++ Tools - Message filters. Select filter and click edit. If the secondary display used is smaller than the primary, the dailog is truncated. I would say this is a regression remaining after Bug 1633306 . Or a preexisting bug that is exposed by the fix.

summary: Typo in header of class MozMailMultiEmailheaderfield
bug_report_text: The header of class MozMailMultiEmailheaderfield reads: /** * The mail-multiemail-headerfield widgets shows multiple emails. It collapses long rows and allows * toggling the full view open. This widget is typically used in the message header pane to show * addresses for To, Cc, Bcc, and any other addressing type header that can contain more than one * mailbox. * * extends {MozXULElement} */ But it acutally binds the XUL element mail-multi-emailheaderfield So the second dash is placed wrong. Also it would make life easier for addon developers if the renaming would be mentioned at https://developer.thunderbird.net/add-ons/tb68/changes

summary: Cards View: Basic fix to existing tooltip that shows which tags a message has
bug_report_text:
Right now, the tooltip has a list of tags with no space for separation.

At a minimum, there should be commas with a space in between.

Update: We may explore an unordered list in the future, but this is beyond the scope of this ticket.

summary: Double separator in context menu before extension items with submenu / multiple items
bug_report_text:
This is a continuation of bug #1787130, which was not fixed in all cases.

Steps to reproduce:
* Web extension which adds 2 context menu items to the same menu.
* Hover over the menu item.

Actual result:
* In the moment the submenu appears, a second, double menu separator appears above.

Expected result:
* Only 1 menu separator

Code for reproduction:
```
  let item1 = browser.menus.create({
    contexts: ["folder_pane"],
    onclick: () => alert("1"),
    title: "First menu item",
  });
  let item2 = browser.menus.create({
    contexts: ["folder_pane"],
    onclick: () => alert("2"),
    title: "Second menu item",
  });
```
text: Double separator in context menu before extension items with submenu / multiple items [SEP] This is a continuation of bug #1787130, which was not fixed in all cases. Steps to reproduce: * Web extension which adds 2 context menu items to the same menu. * Hover over the menu item. Actual result: * In the moment the submenu appears, a second, double menu separator appears above. summary: Implement a "clear" button for the (quick) search bar custom element
bug_report_text:
+++ This bug was initially created as a clone of Bug #1835687 +++

Please restore the "cancel button" in the QFB search field:

This button is very important to clear the field and also cancel any search in progress.

Compare the UX efficiency before and after bug 1835687:
Before: One click clears and cancels. One action.
After: Click after the text (error-prone), drag to the start (error-prone), delete. Three actions.
Positioning of the caret and dragging it is error-prone, and missing to remove all of the text will trigger a new search.

summary: mailTabs.getSelectedMessages: Error: An unexpected error occurred
bug_report_text:
Steps to reproduce:

`mailTabs.getSelectedMessages` may throw unexpected error. The problem exist in thunderbird-103.0a1 as well.
- Load the following add-on. 
- Switch to a 3pane mail tab
- Click on account entry in the folder list so no folder should be selected
- Click on the browserAction
- Inspect messages in console

`manifest.json`
```json
{
	"manifest_version": 2,
	"name": "Get Selected Messages Test",
	"version": "0.1",
	"permissions": [ "messagesRead" ],
	"background": { "scripts": [ "gsm-bg.js" ] },
	"browser_action": { "default_title": "Get Selected Messages" }
}
```
`gsm-bg.js`
```js
"use strict";

async function gsmGetMessage(tab) {
	console.log("getDisplayedMessages",
		await browser.mailTabs.getSelectedMessages(tab.id));
}

browser.browserAction.onClicked.addListener((tab) => { gsmGetMessage(tab); });
```


Actual results:

Extension console
```
Uncaught (in promise) Error: An unexpected error occurred
    gsmGetMessage moz-extension://f923b563-c38c-4bcf-af0d-5c83841fd52f/gsm-bg.js:5
    <anonymous> moz-extension://f923b563-c38c-4bcf-af0d-5c83841fd52f/gsm-bg.js:8
undefined
    gsmGetMessage moz-extension://f923b563-c38c-4bcf-af0d-5c83841fd52f/gsm-bg.js:4
    AsyncFunctionThrow self-hosted:683
    (Async: async)
    <anonymous> moz-extension://f923b563-c38c-4bcf-af0d-5c83841fd52f/gsm-bg.js:8
    apply self-hosted:2716
    applySafeWithoutClone resource://gre/modules/ExtensionCommon.jsm:627
    fire resource://gre/modules/ExtensionChild.jsm:812
    withHandlingUserInput resource://gre/modules/ExtensionCommon.jsm:118
    recvRunListener resource://gre/modules/ExtensionChild.jsm:815
    recvRunListener self-hosted:1162
    _recv resource://gre/modules/ConduitsChild.jsm:82
    receiveMessage resource://gre/modules/ConduitsChild.jsm:188
```
thunderbird error console
```
can't access property "getSelectedMsgHdrs", folderDisplay.view.dbView is null ext-mailTabs.js:314
    getSelectedMessages chrome://messenger/content/parent/ext-mailTabs.js:314
    getSelectedMessages self-hosted:1162
    result resource://gre/modules/ExtensionParent.jsm:1045
    withCallContextData resource://gre/modules/ExtensionParent.jsm:528
    result resource://gre/modules/ExtensionParent.jsm:1044
    withPendingBrowser resource://gre/modules/ExtensionParent.jsm:538
    result resource://gre/modules/ExtensionParent.jsm:1043
    callAndLog resource://gre/modules/ExtensionParent.jsm:996
    recvAPICall resource://gre/modules/ExtensionParent.jsm:1042
    AsyncFunctionNext self-hosted:679
```


Expected results:

Empty array returned by `mailTabs.getSelectedMessages`.

Bonus for 103.0a1: try to close error console and open it again.

summary: Quick filter button on the toolbar is disabled when viewing search results as a list
bug_report_text:
User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:109.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/119.0

Steps to reproduce:

First I've place a "Quick Filter" button on the toolbar. Then I did a global search, got some results back and then clicked on "Show results as list".


Actual results:

The Quick Filter button I have placed on the toolbar is disabled, however the button that's on the "Message List Header" remains functional.


Expected results:

Both buttons should be functional. 

Found this because I wanted to hide the "Message List Header" (losing the message count, unfortunately).

Tested this both on the stable release and on the daily release (121.0a1).

summary: Some options from Settings are skipped when navigating using Tab button
bug_report_text: **Affected versions** * 139.0a1(20250414102533) * 138.0b3(20250416013030) **Affected platforms** * Windows 10 * Windows 11 * macOS 14 * Ubuntu 24 **Steps to reproduce** 1. Launch TB. 2. Login with valid credentials. 3. Go to Settings. 4. summary: Pane separator re-size handle missing in Message Filter window
bug_report_text: User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/70.0.3538.102 Safari/537.36 Steps to reproduce: 1 Access Message Filter tool by selecting Tools - Message Filters (MF window appears) 2 Select filter name to edit 3 Select Edit (Filter Rules window appears) Actual results: Centre pane separator panel (labelled "Perform these actions:") is immovable due to missing re-size grip widget. The two panes of the filter window are now equal fixed-size and do not allow modification of the display area to match contents (UI functionality modification) Expected results: Centre pane separator should contain re-size grip widget to allow pane re-sizing.

summary: Changing account name in account settings does not propagate to account's parent folder until restart of TB (also Tab title and Account central title not instantly updated)
bug_report_text:
From my smoketesting of 111.0b2 (64-bit), Win10.

Regression. This was working in TB 102, except for bug 1734671.
Confusing and inconvenient having to restart TB just for changing account name.

STR
- Right-click on an account's parent folder in folder pane > Settings
- From account settings, change the account name
- Focus 3-pane tab and verify if account name has been updated
  - on account's parent folder in folder pane
  - in tab title of selected parent folder
  - in title of account central content pane

Actual
- account name does **not** get immediately updated
  - on account's parent folder in folder pane (only after restart - clumsy and weird!)
  - in tab title of selected parent folder (only after selecting another folder and back)
  - in title of account central content pane (only after selecting another folder and back)

Expected
- Update account name everywhere, instantly after focus-out on the Account name field in the account settings
- Do not require a restart to update account's parent folder name in folder pane

summary: Merge aboutPreferences.css and preferences.css into preferences.css
bug_report_text: Actually we have preferences.css and aboutPreferences.css which styles the prefs. This comes from the times we had the prefs in dialog and the in-content prefs. Now with only the in-content prefs we don't need both files, especially some rules in aboutPreferences.css needed to revert some rules from preferences.css to show correctly in the prefs tab. Now we can merge all rules in preferences.css.

summary: Content process 9649 isn't responsive while sending "DevToolsProcessParent:addOrSetSessionDataEntry" request
bug_report_text: Created attachment 9487931 Screenshot 2025-05-15 at 12.41.08.png **Affected versions** * 138.0.1(20250512083454) **Affected platforms** * macOS 14 * macOS 15 **Steps to reproduce** 1. Launch TB. 2. Login with valid credentials. 3. Go to your Inbox folder. 4. Select any email. 5. Open Error Console. **Expected result** * No errors should be displayed. **Actual result** * ``` Content process 9649 isn't responsive while sending "DevToolsProcessParent:addOrSetSessionDataEntry" request. DevTools will ignore this process for now. DevToolsProcessParent.sys.mjs:320:17 timeout resource://devtools/server/connectors/js-process-actor/DevToolsProcessParent.sys.mjs:320 notify resource://gre/modules/Timer.sys.mjs:49 Content process 9649 is responsive again. DevTools resumes operations against it. DevToolsProcessParent.sys.mjs:330:21 sendQuery resource://devtools/server/connectors/js-process-actor/DevToolsProcessParent.sys.mjs:330 ```

summary: gloda.index_msg: Observed header that claims to be gloda indexed but that gloda has never heard of during compaction. // IndexMsg.sys.mjs:1349:21
bug_report_text: Steps to reproduce: Start TB. Move around some Mails between different IMAP accounts and local folders. Clear junk- and trash folders. Starting "compacting". Actual results: Error console is full of messages like 23:28:43.707 gloda.index_msg: Observed header that claims to be gloda indexed but that gloda has never heard of during compaction. In folder: imap:// [...] /INBOX sketchy key: 10645 subject: [...] IndexMsg.sys.mjs:1349:21 multiple hundreds of different "sketchy keys" with meaningfull subjects where listed. High CPU and Harddisc activities harden working for other processes. Expected results: Index should not be damaged by normal operation. At least it should inform the user, that the index needs to become repaired - or repair the index after asking for users okay. High CPU and Harddisc activities should be time reduced.

summary: Incorrect unread/total message counts in favorite/unread folder modes
bug_report_text: __Steps to reproduce:__ 1. Have two folders, one a subfolder of the other, both containing some unread messages, and mark them as favorites. 2. In favorite folder mode, collapse the parent folder. 3. Enable compact view. 4. Disable compact view. 5. Ensure the parent folder is collapsed. 6. Unmark the child folder as a favorite. __Expected result:__ After steps 3 and 6, the correct unread message count for the parent folder is shown, not including its subfolders. __Actual result:__ After steps 3 and 6, the unread message count shown includes the subfolders' counts.

summary: Menu bar is turned off on update from 128.12.0esr to 140.0.1esr because of migration error
bug_report_text: Affected versions 140.0.1esr(20250709213912). summary: Quadruple focus indication in Unified toolbar customization
bug_report_text:
When using Unified toolbar customization with keyboard, identifying focus soon becomes a guessing game, because focus indication is too sticky on various UI sections. Three different types of focus/active borders are pretty confusing, and the actual element which has focus doesn't stand out enough if it's a button.

STR

1. Right-click on Unified Toolbar > Customize…
2. Press `Tab` six times (sic)
3. Check the dialog UI for indication of focus

Actual result (with Dark Theme)
- Faint dotted blue border is used as focus indication on buttons (you can use cursor keys to move focus from one button to another within the active group).
- 4 different elements now have focus border decorations simultaneously, 3 dotted and 1 solid (see screenshot).
- On the way, huge section focus on `Available for Mail Space only` and `Available for all Spaces` - this doesn't help much.

Expected result
- Focused buttons should have the regular, clear focus indication (solid blue border).
- Focus indication should not stick around on elements which no longer have focus.
- No need for section focus imho. Instead, (initially) the first button of that section should have a clear, solid blue focus border (like the search field), and cursor keys should move that solid focus border to the other buttons. If active section border hint is wanted, it should be much de-emphasized so that the actual focus on the selected button stands out.

summary: Messages are often displayed scrolled down instead of starting at the top
bug_report_text: Steps to reproduce: In the Classic View, with the folder and message pane shown, click on a message line in the folder view. Expected result: The selected message is shown in the message pane, with the top of the message shown. Actual result: Several time the initial display for the message has been scrolled down. This has happened with both e-mail message and in RSS feeds. I have identified no definite patterns, but I suspect the problem only exists for the first view of a particular mention.

summary: Hidden Quick Filter Bar keeps coming back after restarting TB
bug_report_text:
STR:

1. Click View > Toolbars > Quick Filter Bar in the menu bar
2. Exit Thunderbird
3. Start Thunderbird

Expected:

The quick filter bar is disabled after step 1 and remain disabled after step 3.

Actual:

The quick filter bar is disabled after step 1 but is enabled again after step 3.

Tested in 112.0a1 (2023-02-21) on macOS 13.2.

summary: option checkmarks in hamburger button have different background
bug_report_text: Thunderbird 60.8 has a solid blue background for any checkmarks in Options using the hamburger button. Thunderbird 68.0 (build id 20190816191614) doesn't . It displays checkmarks for the menu bar, mail toolbar etc. using the same background as the rest of the window. I don't know if this is incorrect, I'm merely reporting it because its different behavior and doesn't match what I see in tools -> options (which is similar to what Firefox does) . Its a minor cosmetic difference. I'm testing using different profiles for each version. Both are using the default theme and don't have a chrome\userChrome.js

summary: Some menu button items heights are too small
bug_report_text: Created attachment 9065334 screenshot.png See attached screenshot. Some of the items in the menu button have reduced hit making them appear blank and difficult to trigger. This is in Thunderbird Daily 68.0a1 2019-05-15 64-bit on Debian 9 with Gnome 3.30.2. Thunderbird Beta 67.0b2 and Thunderbird Release 60.6.1 do not replicate this problem so I can only assume its a regression.

summary: Too small size of message when searching messages (Polish)
bug_report_text: Created attachment 9117468 Screenshot_33.png User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/79.0.3945.88 Safari/537.36 Steps to reproduce: Open TB 68.3.1 x64, go to Received folder, open search by Ctrl+Shift+K, type something that does not exist. Actual results: There is banner in message list that nothing has been found, but the banner is too small to show full text. See attached picture. Expected results: Banner size should be at least for the size of the text.

summary: Folder Pane loses selected highlight when focus lost [Linux]
bug_report_text:
Observed on Thunderbird 115.0a1 2023-05-17

OS: Linux64 (Arch/GNOME)
Thunderbird is set to use the "System Theme"
GNOME is using "Adwaita-dark" for Legacy Applications

STR
- Select a folder in the folder pane
- Select a message in that folder

Expected
- Some indication of what folder is active should be visible in the folder pane

Actual
- Nothing of the sort. See video

This only seems to be a problem with the System theme on Linux. Dark & Light Themes are okay. Windows looked good as well

summary: The display of the quota in the status bar no longer works
bug_report_text:
User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/111.0.0.0 Safari/537.36

Steps to reproduce:

1. Click on a mail folder (for example the inbox). 


Actual results:

Previously if the quota exceeded the value of mail.quota.mainwindow_threshold.show, the quota bar was displayed.
In addition, we always saw the number of mail in the folder.


Expected results:

Recover the display of these items.

If we use the development tool and call UpdateStatusMessageCounts(GetSelectedMsgFolders()[0]) for mails and UpdateStatusQuota(GetSelectedMsgFolders()[0]) for quota; the information is back.

There must be a problem with the function that is supposed to call and update its elements. This seems to be _updateContextDisplay of folderDisplay.js


This bug is a bug in Beta.

summary: Thunderbird window resizes itself when content of status bar at bottom of window is too wide
bug_report_text: At least in some circumstances -- not sure of exact details -- Thunderbird resizes its own window, making it wider, when the contents of the status bar are too wide to fit in the window at its previous size. I have the following add-ons which display content in the status bar: ShowFolderSize, gContactSync (though its status bar content is _invisible_, it's there and takes up width), Send Later, Expression Search / Google Mail UI, and ThunderSec. The last add-on, ThunderSec, is the one that triggers window width changes at seemingly random times, since its content in the status bar is dynamic: it pops up messages when doing DNS lookups to check on the security status of messages, indicating how many DNS lookups remain to be done. I feel like the status bar contents being wider than the window should not cause the window width to change. Rather, I feel like the excess content should just be cut off at the right side of the window. FYI, this happens to me on Linux, in particular Ubuntu 18.10. Don't know about other platforms.

summary: Green "Update available" icon shown in tab bar has no tooltip and does nothing
bug_report_text: Created attachment 9179478 Screenshot 1: Green update icon without tooltip and not responding to click Seen on 78.2.2 (32-Bit)-DE STR Updates setting is on automatic. An auto-update on release-channel is pending (dot release). Actual As seen in attached screenshot: Green Update available icon shown in tab bar - has no hanger (maybe clicked away days ago) - has no tooltip - does nothing on click This might be irritating for average users, as they won't know the meaning of the icon, and there's no easy way to find out. About TB correctly shows the button "To finish the update, restart Thunderbird" (translated from German). Expected - green update icon should have tooltip - clicking the update icon should do something (e.g. to re-show the hanger usually attached to it)

summary: Blue "unseen" indicator on folders makes the folder name unreadable in Mojave Dark Mode
bug_report_text: With the Mojave OS set to Dark Mode, the blue "unseen" folder names are unreadable in the folder list, because the dark blue blends in with the dark background. I suggest when in Dark Mode the folder names should be a brighter blue color to provide a little more contrast (just like the other folder names become white instead of black when in dark mode). The blue when not in Dark Mode is fine how it is.

summary: Custom columns added by ThreadPaneColumns.addCustomColumn` do not have tooltips when the text is too long for display
bug_report_text: A custom column added by `ThreadPaneColumns.addCustomColumn` does not have any tooltips for the fields within the columns, unlike the standard built in columns. This means that when an add-on adds a column, it is not possible for the user to see the full content of a column where the text is too wide for the visible area.

summary: width and hight to small for WX toolbar icons in messenger.css
bug_report_text: In messenger.css is the size for toolbar buttons: ```css .toolbarbutton-1 .toolbarbutton-icon { width: 18px; height: 18px; } ``` But the size for WX browserActions is: /* Rules to help integrate WebExtension buttons */ ```css .webextension-action > .toolbarbutton-badge-stack > .toolbarbutton-icon { height: 16px; width: 16px; } ``` The resulting WX icons size is 16 (16 px - 2x1 px padding = 14 px) instead of 18 px (18 px - 2x1 px padding = 16 px).

summary: UI glitches in Preferences tab subdialogs
bug_report_text: Created attachment 9130645 font-dialog.png I stumbled upon a couple of UI glitches in the subdialogs of the Preferences tab on daily. The `Fonts & Encoding` subdialog (_General > Language & Appereance > Fonts & Colors > Advanced_) shows a blown out font size in the first row, and the content doesn't fit in the constraints of the dialog. The other issue is related to the `:focus` effect on a `richlistbox` noticeable in the `Daily Language Settings` sudialog (_General > Language & Appereance > Language > Set Alternatives..._). This issue presents itself in other dialogs, but is not always consistent.

summary: Global search box should have a blue focus border when focused
bug_report_text: STR 1) main 3 pane, click to focus Global Search input box ("Search...") 2) for comparison, click to focus Quick Filter input box ("Filter these messages...") Actual 1) global search box has cursor, but no other visual indication of focus (this bug) 2) Quick filter box has cursor and blue border around the input box Expected 1) global search input should have blue border when focused, same as quick filter Also, whilst the 1px blue border is very elegant, it's pretty hard to spot. Maybe it doesn't have to be as striking as the current focus in account-settings-Auto-BCC-input (2px focus border, 2px light-blue shadow), but some middle ground between the two extremes would be good, e.g. complementing the 1px solid focus border with some sort of lighter shadow which emphasises where the focus is. A clear indication of focus is definitely helpful between dozens of elements on the screen.

summary: Cannot change View Headers in Standalone Message Window
bug_report_text:
User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:109.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/117.0

Steps to reproduce:

Right click with mouse on a message and the Open Message in New Window. Try to use View Headers All (current is View Headers Normal). I cannot select "All" by using keyboard or mouse input.

Note: View Headers All works fine when using Ctrl-O or double-clicking on a message to open it in a tab instead of in a new window.


Actual results:

Viewing Headers All did not happen. Nothing happens when All is selected. The headers view remains on Normal.


Expected results:

All headers should be displayed.

summary: Update the Account Manager UI to match the Preferences Tab UI
bug_report_text: Now that bug 1610445, we finally have the Account Manager living in a tab. With that said, it looks pretty ugly. Let's update the UI to match what we did with the Preference Tab and keep everything consistent. Some prototyping needs to happen for the sidebar in order to properly handle those submenu elements and the button at the bottom, but for the rest, we should stick to what we have in the preferences tab.
